problem.summary: Given row-major logits and one uniform random value per row, select the top-k logits, compute a temperature-scaled softmax over those candidates, and return one sampled token id. The baseline assigns one thread to each row. The optimized seed assigns one block to each row, builds per-thread candidate lists, and merges them with a shared-memory block reduction.
constraints: The seed kernels support float logits and k <= 8
Top-k candidates are sorted by descending logit with lower token id winning ties
The sampling distribution is softmax(top_k_logits / temperature)
NaN handling is not part of this seed contract

verification.method: CUDA harness compares top-k indices, raw top-k values, top-k probabilities, and sampled ids against a CPU reference with deterministic tie handling. Ported from the CUDA corpus; rerun on ROCm hardware before marking passed.

curation.notes: Ported from the CUDA corpus on 2026-05-20. CUDA-origin timing artifacts were removed; rerun on ROCm hardware before making performance claims.
